skill.name: pytest
skill.description: Guide for writing, running, and organizing Python tests with pytest.
skill.summary: Create practical pytest tests with fixtures, parametrization, async tests, markers, and common testing patterns.
skill.author: bossjones

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 46 issues, but review found a Markdown pytest guide with no executable skill automation or prompt injection text. The eval example is a real copy-paste risk because it demonstrates dynamic code execution in a test. Command, environment, weak crypto, and reconnaissance findings are false positives from code fences or toy examples.

medium_findings.title: Unsafe eval Example in Documentation
medium_findings.description: The pytest parametrization example evaluates string expressions with eval(). The inputs are hardcoded, but the pattern teaches dynamic code execution and could be copied into tests that process untrusted data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The eval call is direct and present in a user-facing code example. Risk is reduced because the skill is documentation-only and the sample inputs are fixed strings.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Are Not Executed by the Skill
low_findings.description: The external command findings are Markdown code fences and inline pytest or uv examples. They document normal local test commands and do not show command construction from user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are documentation examples inside Markdown fences or inline code. No executable wrapper, hook, or generated script is present.

low_findings.title: Toy Environment Variable Example
low_findings.description: The environment access findings refer to monkeypatch.setenv using a placeholder API_KEY in a unit test. No real secret is read, persisted, or transmitted.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API_KEY value is a fixed test string in a pytest monkeypatch example. I found no network call or secret collection behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Scanner Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography match is the word powerful in the description. Reconnaissance matches occur in ordinary API and exception testing examples, not system discovery logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive text, sample user IDs, or pytest.raises examples. They do not reference cryptographic algorithms or host reconnaissance commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dynamic Code Execution Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation includes an eval() assertion in a parametrized test. This should be replaced with a safer arithmetic parser or explicit expected values.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is clearly present and security-relevant. The confidence is below very high because it appears in documentation rather than executable skill code.

content.value_statement: Python teams often need clear test patterns for fixtures, async code, APIs, and databases. This skill gives concise pytest examples for common testing tasks.
actual_capabilities: Shows basic pytest test structure and assertions.
Explains fixtures, fixture scopes, setup, and teardown.
Demonstrates parametrized tests and parametrized fixtures.
Covers async tests and async fixtures with pytest markers.
Provides examples for API, database, error, and marker testing.
limitations: It is a documentation skill and does not run tests automatically.
It does not inspect a project before suggesting patterns.
Some examples use placeholder functions that users must replace.
The eval example should be avoided for untrusted input.

use_cases.title: Add Unit Tests to a Python Module
use_cases.description: Use the basic structure, assertions, and fixture examples to create repeatable unit tests.

use_cases.title: Organize Shared Test Setup
use_cases.description: Use fixture scopes and conftest guidance to share setup across test files without duplication.

use_cases.title: Test Web and Database Code
use_cases.description: Use the API and database examples as starting points for integration-style tests.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Test
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple pytest test.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write pytest tests for this Python function. Use clear assertions and include one normal case and one edge case.

prompt_templates.title: Add Fixtures
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor these tests to use pytest fixtures. Keep setup readable and choose the right fixture scope.

prompt_templates.title: Parametrize Test Cases
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert these repeated pytest tests into parametrized tests. Avoid eval and keep each case easy to understand.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Test Suite
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a project testing plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a pytest test suite for this Python service. Include unit, API, database, async, marker, and fixture recommendations.

output_examples.input: Ask for tests for a small Python utility.
output_examples.output: A concise pytest test file outline with descriptive test names, normal cases, edge cases, and clear assertions.

output_examples.input: Ask how to reduce repeated setup across tests.
output_examples.output: A fixture-based structure that separates setup from assertions and explains which fixture scope to use.

output_examples.input: Ask for a backend testing strategy.
output_examples.output: A practical plan covering API client tests, database isolation, async behavior, markers, and failure assertions.

best_practices: Use explicit expected values instead of executing strings with eval.
Keep fixtures small and name them after the resource they provide.
Separate unit, integration, slow, and async tests with clear markers.
anti_patterns: Do not hide complex behavior inside broad fixtures.
Do not depend on real production services in routine tests.
Do not copy placeholder credentials into real application configuration.

faq.question: Does this skill run pytest for me?
faq.answer: No. It explains pytest patterns and commands, but it does not execute tests.

faq.question: Can it help with async tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes async test and async fixture examples using pytest markers.

faq.question: Does it cover fixtures?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers basic fixtures, setup and teardown, and fixture scopes.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance is suitable for Claude, Claude Code, and Codex prompts.

faq.question: Is the eval example safe?
faq.answer: It is safe only for fixed toy inputs. Avoid eval when input can come from users or files.

faq.question: Does it include database testing examples?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows an in-memory SQLAlchemy test fixture pattern for database tests.
